pi_items.source: Mathematical verification using standard epidemiological sample size formula: n = (z/E)² × p × (1-p) with finite population correction. For Survey A: Population 250,000, Expected Frequency 15%, Margin of Error 5%, Design Effect 1.0, Confidence Level 95% (z=1.96). Formula: n = (1.96/0.05)² × 0.15 × 0.85 = 195.91 → FPC correction → 195.76 ≈ 196.

Mathematical verification using standard epidemiological sample size formula. For Survey B: Population 5,000, Expected Frequency 50%, Margin of Error 3%, Design Effect 1.0, Confidence Level 95% (z=1.96). Formula: n = (1.96/0.03)² × 0.50 × 0.50 = 1067.07 → FPC correction = 1067.07/(1+(1067.07-1)/5000) = 879.54 ≈ 880.

Mathematical verification using standard epidemiological sample size formula with design effect. For Survey C: Population 12,000, Expected Frequency 85%, Margin of Error 3%, Design Effect 2.0, Confidence Level 99% (z=2.576). Formula: n = (2.576/0.03)² × 0.85 × 0.15 = 939.94 → FPC correction = 871.73 → design effect × 2.0 = 1743.47 → rounds to 1744.
Reasonable tolerance value given standard rounding in sample size calculations. All three computed values fall within ±10 of the expected values (Survey A: 0, Survey B: 0, Survey C: 1 difference).
Cross-reference with task description (line 6): 'Create a text file at C:\Users\Docker\Documents\survey_sample_sizes.txt'. The metadata output_path matches the task requirement exactly.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires calculating sample sizes for three epidemiological surveys using Epi Info 7 StatCalc's Population Survey calculator. Expected sample sizes are: Survey A (250K population, 15% frequency, 5% error, 95% CI) = 196; Survey B (5K population, 50% frequency, 3% error, 95% CI) = 880; Survey C (12K population, 85% frequency, 3% error, design effect 2.0, 99% CI) = 1744. These values have been verified through independent mathematical calculation using the standard epidemiological sample size formula with finite population corrections: n = (z/E)² × p × (1-p) with adjustment for population size and design effects. The tolerance of 10 is appropriate for rounding differences. Output should be saved to C:\Users\Docker\Documents\survey_sample_sizes.txt.
